checkedCadence: Weekly source check; urgent changes can be added through the Signal Intel Desk daily task.
method: Sourced, dated estimates only. No fake live counters. Red counters show figures or estimate markers only; titles and explanations render underneath. Where a reliable single estimate is not available, the counter uses an estimate-status value such as EST. SOURCE-SPLIT or EST. UNDERCOUNT.

panels.title: Gaza / Israel War
panels.description: Estimated deaths must be updated from OCHA, health-ministry reporting as cited by major wires, UN commission material, and court/inquiry records. Keep deaths, child deaths, injuries, displaced, famine, and contested official responses separate.
panels.sourceLabel: OCHA OPT / OHCHR / UN Commission / Reuters

panels.title: Ukraine War
panels.description: OHCHR verified civilian deaths are a conservative baseline and are lower than the likely real toll. Keep civilian deaths, injuries, military losses, missing persons, displacement, and infrastructure attacks separate.

panels.title: Sudan / Darfur
panels.description: Sudan estimates vary sharply and verified counts are likely severe undercounts. Track verified killings, mass-casualty reports, displacement, famine warnings, hospital attacks, siege conditions, and humanitarian access collapse separately.
panels.sourceLabel: OHCHR Sudan / OCHA / UNHCR / WFP / AP

panels.title: Myanmar Conflict
panels.description: Track civilian deaths, airstrikes, displacement, political detention, ethnic conflict, aid restriction, and humanitarian access. Estimate must be replaced with a sourced figure when the weekly watch finds a stronger public dataset.
panels.sourceLabel: UN OHCHR / OCHA / UNHCR / ACLED where available

panels.title: Yemen War / Humanitarian Collapse
panels.description: Yemen deaths include direct and indirect mortality estimates. Track conflict deaths, excess mortality, hunger, cholera/disease, displacement, port access, blockade claims, and humanitarian operations separately.
panels.sourceLabel: UNDP / OCHA Yemen / WHO / WFP / IPC

panels.title: DR Congo / Great Lakes
panels.description: DRC long-war mortality estimates include direct and indirect conflict effects across years. Track current armed-group violence, civilian killings, sexual violence reports, displacement, disease outbreaks, and mineral/cross-border pressure separately.
panels.sourceLabel: OCHA DRC / UNHCR / MONUSCO / WHO / historical mortality studies

panels.title: Syria War Aftermath
panels.description: Syria estimates vary by source and period. Track deaths, detention, displacement, returns, refugee pressure, sanctions, infrastructure collapse, and humanitarian access as separate evidence categories.
panels.sourceLabel: OCHA Syria / UNHCR / OHCHR / Syrian casualty monitors

panels.title: Global Displacement
panels.description: Estimated forcibly displaced people in the latest UNHCR baseline. Keep refugees, IDPs, asylum seekers, and stateless people separate.

panels.title: Famine / Acute Food Insecurity
panels.description: Estimated people facing high acute food insecurity in the cited hotspot warning lane. IPC phases, famine warnings, siege conditions, and humanitarian access restrictions must be tracked separately.

panels.title: Migration / Irregular Immigration
panels.description: There is no single honest global illegal-immigration counter. Border encounters, irregular crossings, asylum applications, removals, returns, departures, missing migrants, overstays, and estimated unauthorized populations are separate categories.

panels.title: Migration Deaths / Missing Migrants
panels.description: Central Mediterranean deaths/missing from the cited route-specific lane. Mediterranean and desert-route deaths are undercounted and must be updated by route.

panels.title: Sexual Assault / Migrant-Status Data by Country
panels.description: Track sexual-offence data where official statistics identify suspect nationality, immigration status, asylum status, or foreign-born status. Separate police reports, suspects, charges, convictions, victims, unknown status, and per-capita rates. Do not treat nationality as immigration status or individual cases as group guilt.
panels.sourceLabel: National crime offices / courts / police datasets / Eurostat where applicable

panels.title: Vaccines: Damages & Compensation
panels.status: Compensation lane, not raw adverse-event lane
panels.description: Historic VICP compensation figure. Adverse-event reports, compensated claims, pending claims, rejected claims, and causal findings must be separated.
panels.sourceLabel: HRSA VICP / CICP / UK VDPS / VAERS disclaimer

panels.title: Children / Trafficking / Missing
panels.description: CyberTipline reports in 2025. Missing-child cases, online exploitation reports, trafficking indicators, and resolved cases are different categories and must not be merged.

panels.title: Missing Children Cases Supported
panels.description: NCMEC-supported missing-child cases. This is not the same as total missing children worldwide, trafficking victims, or CyberTipline reports.

panels.title: Online Enticement Reports
panels.description: Online enticement reports are suspected online exploitation reports. They are not a missing-child count and not proof of trafficking without investigation.

panels.title: Medical / System Failure
panels.description: Hospital attacks, medicine shortages, blocked aid, disease outbreaks, healthcare worker deaths, excess mortality, and medical-system collapse should be tracked by conflict zone or country, not merged into a global claim.
panels.sourceLabel: WHO / OCHA / national health authorities / court records
